title: Service-Level Objective (SLO) Definition and Multi-Window Multi-Burn-Rate Alerting (availability and latency SLIs, 1h fast burn and 6h slow burn, error-budget posture, multi-service rollups)
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.5.1 duration-first trace anomaly detection, UC-13.5.2 error-rate narratives without formal objectives, UC-13.5.3 trace completeness analytics, UC-13.5.7 RED service-map fusion without SLO windows, or UC-13.5.9 browser Core Web Vitals measurement. Does not automatically change detector programs, sampling policies, or contractual SLAs.
evidence: Saved search obs_uc_13_5_16_mwmbr_slo_burn_rollups with scheduled execution, dashboard drilldowns for burn windows, and weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted observability_evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab tenant, ingest minute buckets where five-minute and one-hour windows simultaneously exceed fast burn multiples for a checkout service with adequate totals; confirm obs_uc_13_5_16_mwmbr_slo_burn_rollups returns critical severity with non-null error_budget_remaining_pct and owner_team from slo_definition.csv within two scheduled runs.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After recovery when rolling six-hour and one-hour indexes return near slo_target with healthy totals, confirm severities return to low or informational and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

dataSources: index=metrics OR index=apm_metrics OR index=otel_metrics for apm.service.request.count, apm.service.request.errors.count, trace.requests, trace.request.errors, spans.count, spans.count.error; index=splunk_apm sourcetype=splunk:apm:metrics for RED rollups; index=signalfx_slo for Observability Cloud SLO detector exports; index=splunk_synthetic sourcetype=synthetic_check_result; index=app_logs for HTTP status-derived success tallies; lookup slo_definition.csv keyed on slo_id; index=cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory for CMDB enrichment
app: Splunk Observability Cloud (APM, SLOs, Synthetics), Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search tier, Splunk Add-on for OpenTelemetry Collector, optional Grafana Cloud and Prometheus Server for upstream counters
spl:
`comment("UC-13.5.16 Multi-window multi-burn-rate SLO engine: APM trace or RED counters, Observability Cloud SLO export, synthetics, log-derived SLI. Tunables: earliest=-7d@h latest=now min_tot_1h=120 min_tot_6h=600 default_slo=0.999")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=apm_metrics OR index=otel_metrics) earliest=-7d@h latest=now
        (metric_name="apm.service.request.count" OR metric_name="apm.service.request.errors.count" OR metric_name="trace.requests" OR metric_name="trace.request.errors" OR metric_name="spans.count" OR metric_name="spans.count.error")
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,'service.name',sf_service,apm_service,service,""))))
      | eval slo_id=coalesce(slo_id_tag,service+"::apm_avail","unscoped_apm_avail")
      | eval mv=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,metric_value,0))
      | eval is_error_metric=if(match(lower(metric_name),"error|errors\\.count|request\\.errors|count\\.error"),1,0)
      | eval req_like=if(is_error_metric=0 AND mv>=0,mv,0)
      | eval err_like=if(is_error_metric=1 AND mv>=0,mv,0)
      | bin _time span=1m
      | stats sum(req_like) AS reqs sum(err_like) AS errs BY _time service slo_id
      | eval good_count=max(0,reqs-errs)
      | eval total_count=reqs
      | eval lane="apm_trace_red_metrics"
      | where total_count>=0
      | fields _time service slo_id good_count total_count lane ]
    [ search index=splunk_apm sourcetype=splunk:apm:metrics earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,'service.name',sf_service,service,""))))
      | eval slo_id=coalesce(slo_id,service+"::red_rollups","unscoped_red")
      | eval reqs=tonumber(coalesce(request_count,span_count,throughput,0))
      | eval errs=tonumber(coalesce(error_count,failed_count,0))
      | bin _time span=1m
      | stats sum(reqs) AS r2 sum(errs) AS e2 BY _time service slo_id
      | eval good_count=max(0,r2-e2)
      | eval total_count=r2
      | eval lane="splunk_apm_red_lane"
      | where total_count>=0
      | fields _time service slo_id good_count total_count lane ]
    [ search index=signalfx_slo earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sf_service,service_name,service,""))))
      | eval slo_id=coalesce(slo_detector_id,slo_name,service+"::sfx_slo","unscoped_sfx")
      | eval good_count=tonumber(coalesce(successful_events,good_events,good_event_count,0))
      | eval total_count=tonumber(coalesce(total_events,valid_events,event_count,0))
      | eval lane="observability_cloud_slo_export"
      | where total_count>0
      | fields _time service slo_id good_count total_count lane ]
    [ search index=splunk_synthetic sourcetype=synthetic_check_result earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(target_service,check_target,service_name,service,""))))
      | eval slo_id=coalesce(slo_key,check_id,service+"::synthetic_avail","unscoped_synthetic")
      | eval ok=if(match(lower(coalesce(result,status,outcome,"")),"success|ok|pass|up"),1,0)
      | bin _time span=1m
      | stats count AS probes sum(ok) AS good_count BY _time service slo_id
      | eval total_count=probes
      | eval lane="synthetic_probe_lane"
      | where total_count>0
      | fields _time service slo_id good_count total_count lane ]
    [ search index=app_logs earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,app_service,k8s_workload,service,""))))
      | eval slo_id=coalesce(slo_route,service+"::http_log_sli","unscoped_logs")
      | eval http_st=tonumber(coalesce(status,http_status_code,http_status,0))
      | where http_st>0 AND http_st<500
      | bin _time span=1m
      | stats count AS total_count BY _time service slo_id
      | eval good_count=total_count
      | eval lane="log_http_success_lane"
      | where total_count>0
      | fields _time service slo_id good_count total_count lane ]
| eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,"unknown_service"))))
| eval slo_id=coalesce(slo_id,"unknown_slo")
| eval good_count=tonumber(coalesce(good_count,0))
| eval total_count=tonumber(coalesce(total_count,0))
| where total_count>0 AND good_count>=0 AND good_count<=total_count
| bin _time span=1m
| stats sum(good_count) AS good_sum sum(total_count) AS tot_sum BY _time service slo_id
| sort 0 service slo_id + _time
| streamstats window=5 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g5 sum(tot_sum) AS t5 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_5m=if(t5>0, round(g5/t5,6), null())
| streamstats window=30 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g30 sum(tot_sum) AS t30 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_30m=if(t30>0, round(g30/t30,6), null())
| streamstats window=60 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g60 sum(tot_sum) AS t60 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_1h=if(t60>0, round(g60/t60,6), null())
| streamstats window=120 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g120 sum(tot_sum) AS t120 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_2h=if(t120>0, round(g120/t120,6), null())
| streamstats window=360 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g360 sum(tot_sum) AS t360 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_6h=if(t360>0, round(g360/t360,6), null())
| streamstats window=1440 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g1440 sum(tot_sum) AS t1440 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_24h=if(t1440>0, round(g1440/t1440,6), null())
| streamstats window=4320 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g4320 sum(tot_sum) AS t4320 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_72h=if(t4320>0, round(g4320/t4320,6), null())
| join type=left max=0 slo_id
    [| inputlookup slo_definition.csv
     | eval slo_id=lower(trim(toString(slo_id)))
     | eval slo_target=tonumber(coalesce(slo_target,0.999))
     | eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,team,"unowned")
     | eval business_priority=coalesce(business_priority,tier,"standard")
     | eval runbook_url=coalesce(runbook_url,"")
     | fields slo_id slo_target owner_team business_priority error_budget_30d_minutes runbook_url ]
| join type=left max=0 service
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service,""))))
      | dedup service
      | fields service env_region cost_center ]
| eval slo_target=tonumber(coalesce(slo_target,0.999))
| eval budget_den=max(0.000001,1-slo_target)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_5m=if(isnotnull(sli_5m), round((1-sli_5m)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_30m=if(isnotnull(sli_30m), round((1-sli_30m)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_1h=if(isnotnull(sli_1h), round((1-sli_1h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_2h=if(isnotnull(sli_2h), round((1-sli_2h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_6h=if(isnotnull(sli_6h), round((1-sli_6h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_24h=if(isnotnull(sli_24h), round((1-sli_24h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_72h=if(isnotnull(sli_72h), round((1-sli_72h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_remaining_pct=round(100*max(0,min(1,(slo_target-coalesce(sli_6h,sli_1h,0))/nullif(slo_target,0)))),2)
| eventstats median(tot_sum) AS med_tot_tick BY _time
| eventstats avg(tot_sum) AS env_tot_baseline BY _time
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_1h,0)>=14.4 AND coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_5m,0)>=14.4 AND coalesce(t60,0)>=120, "critical",
    coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_6h,0)>=6 AND coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_30m,0)>=6 AND coalesce(t360,0)>=600, "high",
    coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_24h,0)>=3 AND coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_2h,0)>=3 AND coalesce(t1440,0)>=2400, "medium",
    coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_72h,0)>=1 AND coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_6h,0)>=1 AND coalesce(t4320,0)>=3600, "low",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "page_fast_burn_invoke_error_budget_policy_freeze_deployments_open_incident",
    severity=="high", "page_slow_burn_review_recent_releases_dependency_health_and_capacity",
    severity=="medium", "open_ticket_root_cause_multi_window_burn_trend_and_synthetic_diff",
    severity=="low", "notify_slack_informational_burn_watch_budget_remaining_dashboard",
    true(), "observe_continue_monitoring_rollups")
| where severity IN ("critical","high","medium","low") AND (coalesce(t60,0)>=120 OR coalesce(t360,0)>=600)
| table _time service slo_id owner_team business_priority slo_target sli_1h sli_6h error_budget_burn_rate_1h error_budget_burn_rate_6h error_budget_remaining_pct severity recommended_action
description: Implements Google Site Reliability Engineering style multi-window multi-burn-rate alerting for availability and latency service level indicators by fusing Splunk APM trace or RED counters, Observability Cloud SLO exports, synthetic checks, and log-derived success tallies, then computing one-hour and six-hour rolling service level index values, error budget burn rates against thirty-day targets, remaining budget posture, and severity tiers that require simultaneous short and long window evidence, enriched with slo_definition.csv and CMDB service inventory for multi-service rollups.
value: Executive and engineering leaders see mathematically grounded burn signals that distinguish a brief catastrophe from a slow leak, which reduces noisy pages while catching real customer pain earlier. Platform teams keep Observability Cloud objectives and Splunk evidence aligned, while service owners receive rows that state current index versus target, remaining budget posture, and a concrete next action instead of a static percentage threshold with no policy context.
implementation: Land APM, RED, Observability Cloud SLO exports, synthetics, and log SLIs into Splunk; maintain slo_definition.csv and CMDB service_inventory. Save obs_uc_13_5_16_mwmbr_slo_burn_rollups on a five-minute cadence with earliest=-7d@h, page on critical or high after min_tot guards, route by owner_team, and archive weekly evidence CSVs.
visualization: Multi-series line chart of sli_1h and sli_6h with slo_target reference band, burn-rate heatmap for one-hour and six-hour windows, single-value tiles for error_budget_remaining_pct, severity-colored table with recommended_action, side panel for synthetic geo status.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Alerts.signature) AS sig latest(Alerts.severity) AS sev count FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-7d@h latest=now BY Alerts.dest
| rename Alerts.dest AS service_key
| join type=left max=0 service_key [| tstats summariesonly=t perc95(Performance.exec_time) AS p95_exec FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-7d@h latest=now BY Performance.process | rename Performance.process AS service_key]
| table service_key sig sev p95_exec
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries for exec_time style metrics correlated to latency objectives; accelerate Alerts summaries when routing SLO burn pages through normalized alert objects tied to service keys.

references.title: Google SRE Workbook — Multi-window multi-burn-rate alerts
Google SRE Book — Service level objectives
Splunk Docs — Create and manage SLOs (Observability Cloud)
Pyrra — Making SLOs with Prometheus manageable
Robust Perception — Multi-window, multi-burn-rate alerts in Prometheus
Splunk Docs — Introduction to Splunk APM
Splunk Docs — Introduction to Splunk Real User Monitoring

knownFalsePositives: Approved maintenance windows recorded only in email can look like surprise burn until the change calendar is authoritative; align CMDB and change tooling before paging. Low-volume midnight batch jobs can create jagged denominators that trip burn math without user impact; require minimum request totals or split batch objectives. Planned chaos engineering fault injection will consume budget on purpose; label environments and suppress vendor-native detectors during signed games. Region failover drills shift traffic and can spike synthetic failures in the origin region while customers remain healthy; annotate drill metadata and compare regional slices. Customer-side throttling tests and partner rate-limit drills can raise error shares without platform defects; confirm traffic attribution before freezing deploys. Log-only lanes miscount when parsers drop fields during parser deploys; monitor ingest health alongside SLI searches. Synthetic geo blocks and captcha walls present as total downtime in one city while others stay green; diversify probes and validate edge DNS. Quota resets and Observability Cloud billing month boundaries may desynchronize internal spreadsheets from detector budgets; reconcile monthly with finance observers. Kubernetes cron skew and double-counted replay topics can inflate totals; dedupe event IDs when log lanes are primary. Grafana Cloud recording rules may lag Prometheus scrape intervals, stretching apparent windows; verify rule group intervals match Splunk bins.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with service owners, site reliability engineers, observability platform leads, and product leadership who translate customer promises into measurable reliability budgets. This use case is the sibling home for multi-window multi-burn-rate alerting math in Splunk search, layered on top of Splunk Observability Cloud SLO detectors, Splunk APM trace-derived counters, RED-style service metrics, synthetic probes, and log-derived availability slices. It is deliberately not UC-13.5.1 trace-duration outlier detection, not UC-13.5.2 error-rate-first narratives without explicit budget math, not UC-13.5.3 trace completeness and orphan-span integrity work, not UC-13.5.7 simultaneous RED service-map fusion without formal objective windows, and not UC-13.5.9 real-user Core Web Vitals measurement. If trace linkage is unreliable, pause strict availability conclusions until UC-13.5.3 stabilizes, because a thin denominator from missing spans can fabricate false calm or false catastrophe.

Begin by separating language precisely. A service level indicator is a measurable good divided by total eligible work in a window. A service level objective is a target for that indicator across an alignment period, commonly thirty rolling days, expressed as a proportion such as ninety-nine point nine percent availability or ninety-five percent of requests faster than three hundred milliseconds. A service level agreement is a contractual promise that may bundle several objectives, support windows, and remedies; your alerting engine should implement objectives and budgets first, then map evidence packs to agreements where legal owners require it. Request-based indicators count discrete attempts and classify outcomes as good or bad. Window-based indicators evaluate whether a slice of time behaved acceptably, which is useful for batch systems where request semantics are awkward; still publish an honest denominator rule so teams cannot hide bad minutes inside quiet clocks.

Composite objectives combine multiple indicators with explicit weights, for example availability plus latency compliance, using AND semantics for customer pain or weighted error budgets when product agrees. Document whether a composite burns a single shared budget or separate budgets per facet, because multi-burn-rate paging against a single budget requires one consolidated burn signal rather than independent pages that double count the same minute. Latency objectives often encode tail promises such as ninety-fifth percentile below three hundred milliseconds; implement latency good events as indicator hits where duration is below the threshold, and keep histogram or percentile export semantics stable across OpenTelemetry and Splunk APM metric bridges. Tracing-based indicators attribute good and bad to server spans, while gateway log indicators attribute to HTTP status and timing at the edge; reconcile the two deliberately because partial sampling on traces can miss rare failures visible in logs, and verbose logs can overweight health checks.

Denominator hygiene is a governance topic, not a footnote. Exclude client faults consistently, for example four hundred ninety-nine when it represents client disconnects, only after you confirm your gateway semantics. When you exclude four xx classes as neutral, document exceptions such as four oh one spikes driven by upstream identity incidents that are truly platform failures for your product story. Canary traffic, health probes, and operator test calls should be labeled and either included with transparency or excluded with a written policy signed by the service owner. Saturation and dependency failures may not raise HTTP five hundreds at the edge while still violating latency objectives; pair availability objectives with latency objectives and dependency health screens.

Error budget policy translates mathematics into behavior. A practical policy freezes risky deploys when remaining budget crosses a quarter of the period budget, escalates review at half, and demands incident commander engagement when fast burn triggers. Pair policy with change calendars so maintenance does not masquerade as mystery burn. False-positive guards matter for low-traffic services: require minimum totals in short windows before fast burn pages, and widen gates for sparse edges using inventory tiers. Sloth and Pyrra represent the modern pattern of generating Prometheus recording and alert rules from declared objectives; even when Splunk remains the system of record, copy the generated windows and burn multiples to keep parity across stacks. In Splunk Observability Cloud, SignalFlow programs can combine streams for objective evaluation; treat combine logic as code with review, version pins, and backtest notes.

Multi-tenancy partitioning should carry service plus region plus environment in keys, not service alone, to prevent cross-region dilution when one geography saturates while another stays healthy. Grafana Cloud and Prometheus Server often hold the raw counters that feed custom_metric_name series; align scrape intervals and staleness rules so rolling windows in Splunk do not average divergent scrape clocks. Kubernetes workload labels should map consistently to service identities used in slo_definition.csv. Legal and privacy reviewers should approve which dimensions appear in pager payloads.

Differentiation recap: formal SLO definitions with thirty-day targets, explicit error budget burn rates, simultaneous short and long windows, multi-service rollups via shared lookup keys, enrichment from slo_definition.csv and CMDB service inventory, and severity mapping inspired by Google multi-window guidance, not generic static thresholds, not pure RED dashboards without objectives, not RUM vitals alone.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A lands APM-aligned counter metrics in metrics-class indexes. Normalize metric_name values such as apm.service.request.count, apm.service.request.errors.count, trace.requests, trace.request.errors, spans.count, and spans.count.error into coherent request and error tallies per minute. Preserve service naming compatible with Splunk APM and OpenTelemetry resource attributes. When dual-writing to Observability Cloud and Splunk Enterprise, keep label cardinality controlled and document which lane is authoritative per environment.

Lane B lands sourcetype=splunk:apm:metrics rows when your bridge emits pre-aggregated RED summaries. Align field names for request_count, error_count, span_count, throughput, and failed_count with the same service keys as Lane A. Deduplicate if both lanes would double count the same requests.

Lane C lands Observability Cloud SLO detector exports in index=signalfx_slo with successful_events, total_events, or equivalent good and total tallies. Map slo_detector_id or slo_name to slo_id keys used in slo_definition.csv so Splunk joins reconcile vendor-native objectives with enterprise governance fields.

Lane D lands synthetic_check_result events in index=splunk_synthetic with explicit pass or fail semantics. Map check_id and target_service to synthetic slices of availability objectives and keep geo diversity, because a single region check may miss DNS or routing failures visible only elsewhere.

Lane E lands application logs in index=app_logs with HTTP status fields. Use conservative filters that count successful responses as good events and omit empty status rows. Treat this lane as a log-based SLI complement, not a replacement for metric denominators, unless product formally adopts log tallies as primary.

Maintain slo_definition.csv with columns slo_id, service, slo_target, error_budget_30d_minutes, owner_team, business_priority, and runbook_url. Maintain CMDB sourcetype=service_inventory with stable service keys and optional env_region for partitioning. HTTP Event Collector tokens and OpenTelemetry Collector pipelines require rate limits, retry visibility, and clock skew monitoring, because skew distorts minute bins and false triggers burn investigations.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as obs_uc_13_5_16_mwmbr_slo_burn_rollups with a five-minute schedule in steady production, dispatch earliest=-7d@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after volume guards pass. Throttle duplicate service and slo_id pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, owner_team, and error_budget_remaining_pct in pager text. Encode runbook_url from slo_definition.csv in ticketing templates.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans five arms for generic metrics indexes, splunk:apm:metrics, signalfx_slo exports, synthetic probes, and log-derived success tallies. coalesce ladders absorb field naming drift. One-minute bins align windows. streamstats implements rolling sums for five minute, thirty minute, one hour, two hour, six hour, twenty-four hour, and seventy-two hour views per service and slo_id. eventstats adds environment-scale volume context for sanity checks. join attaches slo targets and ownership from slo_definition.csv. join attaches CMDB inventory context. case maps multi-window burn pairs into critical, high, medium, and low severities aligned to fast and slow burn patterns. The closing table lists operational columns for dashboards and evidence exports.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.16 Multi-window multi-burn-rate SLO engine: APM trace or RED counters, Observability Cloud SLO export, synthetics, log-derived SLI. Tunables: earliest=-7d@h latest=now min_tot_1h=120 min_tot_6h=600 default_slo=0.999")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=apm_metrics OR index=otel_metrics) earliest=-7d@h latest=now
        (metric_name="apm.service.request.count" OR metric_name="apm.service.request.errors.count" OR metric_name="trace.requests" OR metric_name="trace.request.errors" OR metric_name="spans.count" OR metric_name="spans.count.error")
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,'service.name',sf_service,apm_service,service,""))))
      | eval slo_id=coalesce(slo_id_tag,service+"::apm_avail","unscoped_apm_avail")
      | eval mv=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,metric_value,0))
      | eval is_error_metric=if(match(lower(metric_name),"error|errors\\.count|request\\.errors|count\\.error"),1,0)
      | eval req_like=if(is_error_metric=0 AND mv>=0,mv,0)
      | eval err_like=if(is_error_metric=1 AND mv>=0,mv,0)
      | bin _time span=1m
      | stats sum(req_like) AS reqs sum(err_like) AS errs BY _time service slo_id
      | eval good_count=max(0,reqs-errs)
      | eval total_count=reqs
      | eval lane="apm_trace_red_metrics"
      | where total_count>=0
      | fields _time service slo_id good_count total_count lane ]
    [ search index=splunk_apm sourcetype=splunk:apm:metrics earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,'service.name',sf_service,service,""))))
      | eval slo_id=coalesce(slo_id,service+"::red_rollups","unscoped_red")
      | eval reqs=tonumber(coalesce(request_count,span_count,throughput,0))
      | eval errs=tonumber(coalesce(error_count,failed_count,0))
      | bin _time span=1m
      | stats sum(reqs) AS r2 sum(errs) AS e2 BY _time service slo_id
      | eval good_count=max(0,r2-e2)
      | eval total_count=r2
      | eval lane="splunk_apm_red_lane"
      | where total_count>=0
      | fields _time service slo_id good_count total_count lane ]
    [ search index=signalfx_slo earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sf_service,service_name,service,""))))
      | eval slo_id=coalesce(slo_detector_id,slo_name,service+"::sfx_slo","unscoped_sfx")
      | eval good_count=tonumber(coalesce(successful_events,good_events,good_event_count,0))
      | eval total_count=tonumber(coalesce(total_events,valid_events,event_count,0))
      | eval lane="observability_cloud_slo_export"
      | where total_count>0
      | fields _time service slo_id good_count total_count lane ]
    [ search index=splunk_synthetic sourcetype=synthetic_check_result earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(target_service,check_target,service_name,service,""))))
      | eval slo_id=coalesce(slo_key,check_id,service+"::synthetic_avail","unscoped_synthetic")
      | eval ok=if(match(lower(coalesce(result,status,outcome,"")),"success|ok|pass|up"),1,0)
      | bin _time span=1m
      | stats count AS probes sum(ok) AS good_count BY _time service slo_id
      | eval total_count=probes
      | eval lane="synthetic_probe_lane"
      | where total_count>0
      | fields _time service slo_id good_count total_count lane ]
    [ search index=app_logs earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,app_service,k8s_workload,service,""))))
      | eval slo_id=coalesce(slo_route,service+"::http_log_sli","unscoped_logs")
      | eval http_st=tonumber(coalesce(status,http_status_code,http_status,0))
      | where http_st>0 AND http_st<500
      | bin _time span=1m
      | stats count AS total_count BY _time service slo_id
      | eval good_count=total_count
      | eval lane="log_http_success_lane"
      | where total_count>0
      | fields _time service slo_id good_count total_count lane ]
| eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,"unknown_service"))))
| eval slo_id=coalesce(slo_id,"unknown_slo")
| eval good_count=tonumber(coalesce(good_count,0))
| eval total_count=tonumber(coalesce(total_count,0))
| where total_count>0 AND good_count>=0 AND good_count<=total_count
| bin _time span=1m
| stats sum(good_count) AS good_sum sum(total_count) AS tot_sum BY _time service slo_id
| sort 0 service slo_id + _time
| streamstats window=5 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g5 sum(tot_sum) AS t5 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_5m=if(t5>0, round(g5/t5,6), null())
| streamstats window=30 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g30 sum(tot_sum) AS t30 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_30m=if(t30>0, round(g30/t30,6), null())
| streamstats window=60 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g60 sum(tot_sum) AS t60 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_1h=if(t60>0, round(g60/t60,6), null())
| streamstats window=120 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g120 sum(tot_sum) AS t120 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_2h=if(t120>0, round(g120/t120,6), null())
| streamstats window=360 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g360 sum(tot_sum) AS t360 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_6h=if(t360>0, round(g360/t360,6), null())
| streamstats window=1440 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g1440 sum(tot_sum) AS t1440 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_24h=if(t1440>0, round(g1440/t1440,6), null())
| streamstats window=4320 current=t global=f sum(good_sum) AS g4320 sum(tot_sum) AS t4320 BY service slo_id
| eval sli_72h=if(t4320>0, round(g4320/t4320,6), null())
| join type=left max=0 slo_id
    [| inputlookup slo_definition.csv
     | eval slo_id=lower(trim(toString(slo_id)))
     | eval slo_target=tonumber(coalesce(slo_target,0.999))
     | eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,team,"unowned")
     | eval business_priority=coalesce(business_priority,tier,"standard")
     | eval runbook_url=coalesce(runbook_url,"")
     | fields slo_id slo_target owner_team business_priority error_budget_30d_minutes runbook_url ]
| join type=left max=0 service
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=service_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval service=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service,""))))
      | dedup service
      | fields service env_region cost_center ]
| eval slo_target=tonumber(coalesce(slo_target,0.999))
| eval budget_den=max(0.000001,1-slo_target)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_5m=if(isnotnull(sli_5m), round((1-sli_5m)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_30m=if(isnotnull(sli_30m), round((1-sli_30m)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_1h=if(isnotnull(sli_1h), round((1-sli_1h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_2h=if(isnotnull(sli_2h), round((1-sli_2h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_6h=if(isnotnull(sli_6h), round((1-sli_6h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_24h=if(isnotnull(sli_24h), round((1-sli_24h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_burn_rate_72h=if(isnotnull(sli_72h), round((1-sli_72h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval error_budget_remaining_pct=round(100*max(0,min(1,(slo_target-coalesce(sli_6h,sli_1h,0))/nullif(slo_target,0)))),2)
| eventstats median(tot_sum) AS med_tot_tick BY _time
| eventstats avg(tot_sum) AS env_tot_baseline BY _time
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_1h,0)>=14.4 AND coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_5m,0)>=14.4 AND coalesce(t60,0)>=120, "critical",
    coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_6h,0)>=6 AND coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_30m,0)>=6 AND coalesce(t360,0)>=600, "high",
    coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_24h,0)>=3 AND coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_2h,0)>=3 AND coalesce(t1440,0)>=2400, "medium",
    coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_72h,0)>=1 AND coalesce(error_budget_burn_rate_6h,0)>=1 AND coalesce(t4320,0)>=3600, "low",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "page_fast_burn_invoke_error_budget_policy_freeze_deployments_open_incident",
    severity=="high", "page_slow_burn_review_recent_releases_dependency_health_and_capacity",
    severity=="medium", "open_ticket_root_cause_multi_window_burn_trend_and_synthetic_diff",
    severity=="low", "notify_slack_informational_burn_watch_budget_remaining_dashboard",
    true(), "observe_continue_monitoring_rollups")
| where severity IN ("critical","high","medium","low") AND (coalesce(t60,0)>=120 OR coalesce(t360,0)>=600)
| table _time service slo_id owner_team business_priority slo_target sli_1h sli_6h error_budget_burn_rate_1h error_budget_burn_rate_6h error_budget_remaining_pct severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[obs_uc_13_5_16_mwmbr_slo_burn_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -7d@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch obs_uc_13_5_16_mwmbr_slo_burn_rollups | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance guidance: materialize one-minute rollups into a summary index keyed on service, slo_id, and environment when Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time climbing; widen operational alerts to read summaries while retaining this search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Splunk Observability Cloud SLO views and APM service dashboards for a service you understand. Compare good and total counts per minute to Splunk rows for the same interval. If Splunk lags the UI, verify indexer latency, HEC batching, and collector export intervals.

Validate slo_definition.csv keys by running inputlookup alone and confirming tier-one rows exist. Validate CMDB joins by searching service_inventory and confirming deduped service keys cover payment and authentication paths.

Inject a brief failure in lab, confirm fast burn triggers only when five minute and one hour windows exceed multiples simultaneously with adequate totals, then recover and confirm decay within two scheduled runs.

Negative test: on a quiet window without changes, critical severity should be absent for mature services once min_tot gates are calibrated.

Runbook linkage: tie pages to incident commander checklists, change freeze procedures, synthetic geo maps, and dependency health dashboards.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Brief outage burns thirty percent of monthly budget in ten minutes: confirm incident timeline, capture root cause, and schedule error budget report-out; adjust canary gates if detection lagged reality.

Case 2 — Canary deploy regression detected by one hour fast burn: roll back or forward-fix, compare trace error attributes, and validate feature flags before reopening deploys.

Case 3 — Cron job drives periodic traffic that dilutes windows: move cron traffic to a labeled objective or split indicators so interactive objectives do not inherit batch denominators.

Case 4 — Low-traffic edge service trips false positives: raise min_tot thresholds, downgrade tier in slo_definition.csv, and require synthetics for edges.

Case 5 — Denominator excludes four xx but a spike of four oh one leaks as five xx mislabels: fix mapping at the gateway, reconcile log and trace status, and backfill corrected tallies.

Case 6 — Clock skew between collector and Prometheus distorts minute bins: enforce NTP discipline, align scrape offsets, and pause paging until skew resolves.

Case 7 — SLO target lowered after stakeholder review: update slo_definition.csv, regenerate Sloth or Pyrra rules if mirrored, and rebaseline dashboards.

Case 8 — Feature flag rollout regresses latency objective: isolate cohorts, compare percentile metrics, and roll back flag while latency objective recovers.

Case 9 — Downstream dependency outage drives burn across callers: page owning team for the dependency, shift customer comms, and track budget consumption across composite objectives.

Case 10 — Saturation in one of N regions while others stay healthy: partition alerts by env_region from CMDB, fail traffic over per playbook, and avoid diluting burn with global denominators.

Case 11 — Synthetic check geo-blocked at firewall shows false downtime: validate allow lists, rotate probe locations, and annotate synthetics lane before paging.

Case 12 — Quota error budget reset late after month boundary: reconcile Observability Cloud accounting, align calendar resets in slo_definition.csv, and communicate corrected remaining budget to leadership.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, and inputlookup appear; final table lists thirteen operational columns including error budget posture fields.
grandmaExplanation: We treat reliability like minutes on a monthly phone plan: we watch both a frantic ten-minute spending spree and a slow weekly leak. When the burn pattern matches real customer pain, we call the right leader with a clear next step before the allowance is gone.
hardware: Kubernetes-hosted microservices, Linux or containerized OpenTelemetry Collector pools, optional Prometheus and Grafana Cloud metric pipelines, Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search tier at 9.2 or newer, Observability Cloud realm for APM RUM synthetics and SLO exports.
